##NAMES=Henrysunite
##RRUFFID=R240005
##IDEAL CHEMISTRY=Na_2_(C_2_H_3_O_3_)_2_(H_2_O)
##LOCALITY=Western end of Pusch Ridge in the Santa Catalina Mountains, north of Tucson, Pima County, Arizona, USA
##OWNER=University of Arizona Alfie Norville Gem and Mineral Museum
##SOURCE=Warren Lazar
##DESCRIPTION=Found in cracks and vugs as colorless isolated stout prismatic crystals, associated with albite, chrysocolla, hematite, lazaraskeite, phlogopite, quartz, and stanevansite
##STATUS=The identification of this mineral is confirmed by single-crystal X-ray diffraction and chemical analysis
##URL=https://www.rruff.net/odr/rruff_sample/R240005
##MEASURED CHEMISTRY=(Na_1.93_K_0.03_Ca_0.02_)(C_2_H_3_O_3_)_2_·H_2_O ; Na, K, Ca from microprobe, C, H from structure determination
##DIFFRACTION SAMPLE DESCRIPTION=Powder
##CELL PARAMETERS=a: 6.397 b: 20.312 c: 5.844 alpha: 90 beta: 90 gamma: 90 volume: 759.4 crystal system: orthorhombic
##FILETYPE=Raman RAW
